data.title: SCENTRE GROUP ANNOUNCES CASH TENDER OFFER FOR ANY AND ALL OF ITS OUTSTANDING SUBORDINATED NOTES
data.description: On April 22, 2026, RE1 Limited, acting as the responsible entity and trustee of Scentre Group Trust 2, which is a trust within Scentre Group (ASX: SCG), initiated a cash tender offer.

data.title: Stereotaxis Announces First MAGiC Procedures in the United States
data.description: On April 22, 2026, Stereotaxis (NYSE: STXS) made an announcement that the innovative surgical robotics company had treated the initial patients in the United States with their latest technology, MAGiC™. Stereotaxis, a leading force in minimally invasive endovascular intervention, marked this milestone in their global operations.

data.title: How To Earn $500 A Month From American Express Stock Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description: Before the market opens on Thursday, April 23, American Express Company (NYSE: AXP) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings. Analysts are predicting earnings of $3.99 per share, an increase from $3.64 per share in the same period last year. The expected quarterly revenue is $18.61 billion, up from $16.97 billion last year. A recent report from Benzinga Pro shows that a Morgan Stanley analyst maintained an Equal-Weight rating for American Express on April 16 but reduced the price target from $395 to $385. Investors are also considering the company's dividend potential, with an annual yield of 1.15% and a quarterly dividend of 95 cents per share ($3.80 annually). For those looking to earn $500 monthly from American Express, further details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: What's Going On With Intuitive Surgical Stock Wednesday?
data.description: Intuitive Surgical Inc. (NASDAQ: ISRG) has released positive financial results for the first quarter of 2026, surpassing expectations. The company reported revenue of $2.77 billion, exceeding analyst predictions of $2.62 billion. Additionally, their first-quarter adjusted earnings stood at $2.50 per share, higher than the estimated $2.10 per share. During the conference call, Intuitive Surgical highlighted strong adoption rates for its Da Vinci 5, SP, and Ion systems, with significant procedure growth in the U.S. and Europe, although this was somewhat offset by weaker performance in Asia. Looking ahead, the company now anticipates Da Vinci procedure growth of 13.5%–15.5% for the year 2026, showing an increase from previous projections.

data.title: IBM Likely To Report Higher Q1 Earnings; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: IBM is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on April 22 after the market closes. Analysts predict the company will report earnings of $1.81 per share, an increase from $1.60 per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be around $15.64 billion, up from $14.54 billion in the previous year. IBM has exceeded revenue expectations for five consecutive quarters and in eight out of the last 10 quarters. Closing at $255.68 on Tuesday, IBM shares rose by 0.8%. For more details and analyst ratings, readers can visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Acushnet Holdings Corp. to Announce First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on May 6, 2026
data.description: Acushnet Holdings Corp. (NYSE: GOLF) announced that it will release its first quarter financial results on May 6, 2026 at around 6:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The company will make the results available through its Investor Relations website (http://www.acushnetholdingscorp.com/ir) and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website. Investors and stakeholders can access the information through these platforms.

data.title: Volatus Aerospace adds Major General (Ret’d) Peter Fesler to Advisory Board; Lieutenant-General (Ret’d) Andrew Leslie named Chair.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global e-commerce market is expected to reach $6.5 trillion by 2023, showing significant growth from the current value of $3.5 trillion. The Asia-Pacific region is leading this growth, with China accounting for the largest share of the market at $2.8 trillion. Mobile commerce is also on the rise, with global m-commerce sales projected to reach $3.5 trillion by 2021. North America and Europe are the other two key regions driving the growth of e-commerce, with sales expected to reach $1.4 trillion and $1.3 trillion, respectively, by 2023. The report also highlights the increasing trend of consumers shifting towards online shopping, driven by factors such as convenience, variety, and competitive pricing. These insights suggest a promising future for the e-commerce industry on a global scale.

data.title: 8 in 10 Employees Say They Need AI Training -- After Their Companies Already Rolled Out the Tools
data.description: As AI becomes increasingly prevalent in workplaces, both employees and hiring managers acknowledge that the primary issue lies not within the technology itself, but in the realm of training. In a recent survey conducted in Oklahoma City on April 22, 2026, it was found that artificial intelligence is transforming the nature of tasks in workplaces. However, a significant number of employees express that they have been provided with AI tools without the necessary guidance or education. This highlights a gap in training, with many individuals feeling ill-prepared to effectively utilize AI in their roles.
